title: leymus divaricatus subsp. fasciculatus
intro: Leymus divaricatus subsp. fasciculatus, also known as the bearded wheat grass or bearded grass, is a subspecies of the Leymus divaricatus plant, native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Leymus divaricatus subsp. fasciculatus include reducing inflammation and improving digestion, as well as having potential anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
